reason: Both correctly identify the 3-month grace window (closed mid-April), the requirement to register before suing, and the actual-damages-only limitation for pre-registration infringement. B is slightly stronger: it explicitly ties the pre-suit registration requirement to Fourth Estate, adds practical actionable advice (DMCA takedowns, which don't require registration), and includes an accurate, relevant nuance about the circuit split on continuing infringement that could affect whether post-registration statutory damages apply to ongoing piracy. A is more concise but slightly vaguer on the 'must register before suing' point (folded into a parenthetical) and omits the practical interim step (DMCA) and the continuing-infringement nuance. Neither has factual errors. B's extra content is substantively useful, not padding.
